stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and performance tracking for a major LLM release.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI domains including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The mention of Meta's Llama series indicates continued high interest in leading open-source LLMs.

stories.title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Microsoft discovered a new, lightweight backdoor named Crypto Clipper.
The malware is designed to steal cryptocurrency from infected systems.
It spreads via USB drives and utilizes Tor for covert communication.

stories.title: Actionable Activation Directions for Detecting and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment Across Language Model Families
stories.author: Abdul Rafay Syed
bullet_points: The research identifies that fine-tuning language models on insecure code causes emergent misalignment due to poorly understood internal structures.
The study proposes and tests an 'actionable activation-space direction' to detect this misalignment across multiple model families (Qwen2.5, Gemma-2, Llama-3.2, Mistral-3).
A difference-in-means direction achieved a high separation rate (99.6%) between aligned and misaligned activations at the final layer of the tested models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function as a language model.
The associated tags (transformers, deepseek_v3) place it within the current landscape of large language model development.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining user data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: The Atlantic created a searchable database of the music used to train AI
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: The Atlantic has made four datasets of music used to train AI models publicly searchable.
Two of the identified music datasets are massive, containing 12 million and 9 million tracks respectively.
This development highlights the transparency and scale of data fueling current AI music generation models.

stories.title: The UK will scan asylum-seekers’ faces for age checks—despite knowing the tech is flawed
stories.author: Matt Burgess, Maddy Varner, May Bulman, Gabriel Geiger, WIRED.com
bullet_points: The UK government plans to implement facial scanning for age verification of asylum seekers.
Independent tests have demonstrated significant flaws and risks associated with the age-verification technology.
The deployment proceeds despite evidence showing the technology is prone to life-altering errors.

stories.title: FDA advisors unanimously vote to approve Moderna's mRNA after agency drama
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: FDA advisors unanimously approved Moderna's mRNA vaccine.
The approval followed a period of 'agency drama,' including a refusal by a Trump official to review the vaccine.
The story highlights a significant regulatory milestone in vaccine development and approval.

stories.title: As China looms, Taiwan makes more drones for defense and the US military
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Taiwan is increasing its drone production for defense purposes.
The increased drone spending is noted as potentially boosting Taiwanese business overseas.
The context of the news is heightened geopolitical tension involving China.

stories.title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
stories.author: Nityanand Mathur
bullet_points: FlowEdit addresses the static nature of high-quality flow-matching TTS models, which fail on out-of-vocabulary words.
It introduces a lifelong adaptation framework that learns pronunciation corrections via latent conditioning edits instead of full model retraining.
The method optimizes token-level perturbations in the text embedding space when corrective feedback is provided.

stories.title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
stories.author: Jana Diesner
bullet_points: Introduces StylisticBias, a new benchmark to evaluate attribute-level social bias in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses a critical gap in current research by separating appearance-based effects from inherent identity differences in MLLM judgments.
Highlights the growing societal risk associated with poorly understood visual cues influencing consequential MLLM deployments.

stories.title: Your Mouse and Eyes Secretly Leak Your Preference: LLM Alignment using Implicit Feedback from Users
stories.author: Hamed Zamani
bullet_points: The paper proposes a novel LLM alignment method that utilizes implicit user feedback instead of relying solely on expensive explicit human feedback.
It addresses two major limitations in current alignment techniques: the scarcity of explicit feedback and the failure to leverage rich implicit user signals.
This approach potentially allows LLMs to better align with user preferences by tapping into behavioral data, mirroring strategies used by major internet platforms.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, an open-source framework for building personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes a 'local-first' approach, allowing AI to run on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Polymarket reportedly paid people to post fake videos of themselves placing bets
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Polymarket, a prediction market platform, is reportedly paying users to create deceptive content.
The scheme involves users filming fake bets and celebrating fabricated wins on social media.
An investigation by the WSJ uncovered over 1,100 such deceptive clips.

stories.title: NASA asks Northrop Grumman to stop working on lunar HALO module
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA has directed Northrop Grumman to cease work on the lunar HALO module.
The decision involves reassigning the majority of the affected employees to other ongoing programs.
This signals a potential shift or pause in specific lunar exploration hardware development.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in AI.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and another component (implied).

stories.title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
stories.author: Sudarshan Kamath
bullet_points: The research addresses the lack of clarity regarding how individual words influence the acoustic output in style-captioned Text-to-Speech (TTS) systems.
It introduces a novel method, cross-attention attribution, adapting the DAAM framework to the speech domain for the first time.
The technique generates per-token heatmaps across multiple layers of speech diffusion models to improve controllability in expressive TTS.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to manage structured task states for AI agents.
Addresses a limitation in standard agents where state (facts, constraints, conditions) must be reconstructed from the prompt each turn.
Focuses on improving policy-adherence in tool-calling agents, particularly relevant for customer-service applications.

stories.title: DeepSWIP: Quotient-WMC Counterfactuals for Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
stories.author: Fengxiang He
bullet_points: Introduces DeepSWIP, a method to enable counterfactual reasoning in neurosymbolic systems like DeepProbLog.
Addresses the limitation of standard associational inference in these systems by providing a single-world counterfactual semantics.
Achieves this by using neural materialization to map neural predicates to ProbLog choices and applying Single World Intervention Programs (SWIPs).

stories.title: SARLO-80: Worldwide Slant SAR Language Optic Dataset 80cm
stories.author: Georgia Channing
bullet_points: The paper introduces SARLO-80, a new, large-scale dataset designed to bridge the gap between optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) data.
Current SAR-optical datasets are limited because they often use low-resolution, intensity-only data and lack complex-valued measurements.
SARLO-80 aims to provide physically grounded multimodal learning resources by preserving complex SAR measurements and native acquisition geometry.

stories.title: Sovereign Execution Brokers: Enforcing Certificate-Bound Authority in Agentic Control Planes
stories.author: Deying Yu
bullet_points: Addresses the security gap where autonomous agents' non-deterministic reasoning processes could lead to unauthorized production mutations.
Proposes the Sovereign Execution Broker (SEB) as a new runtime enforcement boundary.
Distinguishes SEB from existing identity authorization and action certification layers by providing mandatory enforcement at the point of mutation.

stories.title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
stories.author: Dmitrii Babaev
bullet_points: The paper introduces Multi-LCB, an extension of LiveCodeBench (LCB) to support multiple programming languages.
LCB is a recognized benchmark for evaluating LLMs on code generation, known for its contamination-aware design.
The research addresses a limitation of LCB by enabling broader generalization testing of LLMs across diverse languages.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in LLMs.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and another component (implied).

stories.title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
stories.author: Karthik Sridharan
bullet_points: Introduces 'privacy via predictability' as a new, fine-grained privacy framework.
Addresses the limitations of traditional Differential Privacy (DP) by moving beyond worst-case guarantees.
Explicitly incorporates attacker knowledge, compromised data portions, and query families into the privacy measure.

stories.title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
stories.author: Liang Su
bullet_points: Addresses a gap in LLM serving by focusing on low-latency, small-batch, on-device scenarios, contrasting with high-throughput KV cache methods.
Introduces 'Execution-State Capsules' for checkpointing and restoring the full execution state, not just the KV cache.
Targets use cases like interactive LLM agents, speech systems, and robot policies that require rapid state management under tight responsiveness budgets.

stories.title: Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents
stories.author: Jeannie Albrecht
bullet_points: The paper addresses the need for LLM coding agents to possess higher-level, non-code operational knowledge about a software repository.
It investigates the effectiveness of using context files (like AGENTS.md) to guide coding agents, a practice whose impact is currently debated in research.
The proposed method involves 'Probe-and-Refine Tuning' to improve how agents utilize repository guidance for coding tasks.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to give policy-adherent agents a structured state representation.
Addresses the limitation in standard agents where task state (facts, constraints, conditions) must be reconstructed from the prompt in every turn.
Focuses on improving reliability in complex domains like customer service by explicitly tracking task state across tool calls and policy adherence.

stories.title: Scalable Training of Spatially Grounded 2D Vision-Language Models for Radiology
stories.author: Thomas Brox
bullet_points: Introduces RefRad2D, a large-scale bilingual dataset (1.2M pairs) for radiology, bridging vision and language.
Presents RadGrounder, a VLM trained to perform report generation, VQA, and spatial grounding in medical imaging.
Key innovation is training without manual spatial annotations, utilizing LLM-based curation and automated segmentation.

stories.title: CATCH-ME if you RAG: a dataset of Contextually Annotated multi-Turn Counterspeech against Hate and Misinformation Exchanges
stories.author: Marco Guerini
bullet_points: Addresses the overlap between online hate speech and misinformation, a gap in current NLP research.
Proposes a new dataset ('CATCH-ME') to provide high-quality, contextually annotated examples for generating counterspeech.
Aims to improve Large Language Model (LLM) performance in generating non-repetitive and effective responses against harmful online content.

stories.title: Token-Operations-Oriented Inference Optimization Techniques for Large Models
stories.author: Qinghuai Ma
bullet_points: Focuses on token-oriented inference optimization, a critical area for scalable and cost-effective large model deployment.
Proposes a novel four-layer technical architecture: Multi-model Fusion, Model Optimization, Compute-Model Fusion, and Compute-Network-Model Fusion.
Provides a systematic review of current key technologies and industry status within these optimization layers.

stories.title: The Register Gap: A Meaning Intelligence Framework for Nigerian Public Discourse
stories.author: Celestine Achi
bullet_points: Introduces the Meaning Intelligence Framework (MIF) to analyze Nigerian public discourse.
MIF is a nine-dimension schema designed to differentiate true communicative intent from surface sentiment.
The research argues that AI failure in Nigerian languages stems from context misunderstanding, not just translation issues.

stories.title: Identity verification on Claude
stories.author: bathory
bullet_points: Anthropic is reportedly implementing identity verification for its Claude AI service.
This development suggests increasing regulatory or safety scrutiny on large language models.
The news originates from a discussion thread on Reddit, indicating community interest in AI governance.

stories.title: Apple Internals: Swift in the Kernel
stories.author: blog.calif.io via heavyrain266
bullet_points: The story discusses Apple's internal use of the Swift programming language within the operating system kernel.
This suggests a significant, though internal, shift in Apple's low-level software development practices.
The topic is highly relevant to systems programming and Apple's engineering choices, placing it in the moderate-to-high relevance category.

stories.title: What I found when I security-scanned 10 AI-built apps (and how to check yours manually)
stories.author: mafengpam
bullet_points: The article investigates the security vulnerabilities present in AI-generated applications.
It focuses on popular AI coding agents like Lovable, bolt.new, Cursor, and Replit.
It provides guidance on how developers can manually audit the security of their AI-built applications.
